recent_calls.summary: Nick called to schedule an emergency service appointment for his air conditioner that is not cooling. The agent documented the issue, escalated it for next business day follow-up, and confirmed Nick's contact details. The call ended with no further requests from Nick.

The user called to request a service related to cooling that can wait until the next business day. The agent attempted to gather more details about the issue and preferred scheduling but the user did not provide further information before ending the call.

Carlotta called about her air conditioner not blowing cold air, which has been an emergency since Friday. She had a scheduled appointment for the day that was missed, and someone is expected to come in the evening. The agent documented the issue as urgent and informed Carlotta that the team will follow up on the next business day.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing themselves and asking for the service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called Budget's after-hours line and inquired if they were speaking to a live person. The virtual assistant clarified it was an automated system and offered to take the user's information for a follow-up on the next business day.

The user called to report that she had not received an invoice to e-sign. The agent acknowledged the issue and assured the user that the team would follow up on the next business day. The call ended with the user confirming no further questions.
The agent greeted the user and asked if the call was about an emergency or routine service, but the user was unsure and did not provide further information before hanging up.

The user, Amy Witt, requested a service call for her air conditioning which is not turning on. She scheduled the service for tomorrow morning between 8 and 10 AM and confirmed her contact details and service address. The agent confirmed all details and informed Amy that the team will follow up on the next business day to confirm the appointment.

Carlotta called to report that her air conditioner was not working and that her scheduled appointment did not occur. She requested emergency service for her air conditioner at her address in Florissant, Missouri, and asked to be contacted around 7:30 AM the next day. The agent confirmed all details and assured her that the request would be prioritized.

The call was an inbound inquiry where the user, Dario from American Home Shield, asked the agent for her name and role. The agent introduced herself as Budget's virtual assistant and offered help, but the call ended shortly after without further interaction.

The user called to follow up on a work order, but the agent informed them that live updates were not available after hours and attempted to take down details. The call ended abruptly shortly after the initial exchange.

The user called after hours to inquire about the exact time of their appointment scheduled for the next morning. The agent informed the user that the exact time could not be confirmed at the moment but assured that the team would follow up the next business day. The user had no further questions and ended the call.

The user called to inquire about the arrival time of a technician scheduled for the next day. The agent informed the user that the exact time was not available after hours but noted the user's preference for an afternoon arrival and assured that the team would confirm the details on the next business day. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further questions.
The user, Alana, called to schedule a plumbing appointment and began explaining the issue to the agent. The call ended shortly after without completing the scheduling process.

The user, Betty, called to clarify the process for signing and scheduling a cooling service appointment. The agent confirmed the service details, noted the user's mobility needs, and assured that the team would contact her on the next business day to finalize scheduling and paperwork. Betty agreed to receive information about home maintenance programs and was satisfied with the assistance provided.

Adam called to report an issue with his outdoor AC unit's fan not spinning and requested to schedule a service appointment. The agent booked an appointment for Wednesday, June 3rd at 4 PM and confirmed the details with Adam. Adam provided feedback rating the experience as a 2 due to background noise and was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Margaret, called requesting customer service for hair washing. The agent attempted to assist but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call lasted 28 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Bill called to order a new AC compressor after a recent service appointment indicated the need for a replacement. The agent confirmed details and offered maintenance program information, which Bill declined. The call ended with Bill providing feedback and requesting to speak with a human agent, after which the call was transferred successfully.

Elaine called to schedule a service appointment for her central air cooling system, specifically for a fan that is not working. The agent confirmed the details, including the home warranty dispatch number and appointment time for 9 AM the next day, and offered information about maintenance programs, which Elaine declined. The call ended with the agent confirming that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her soon.
The user, Anita, called to report an unsolicited call without a message from Budget. The agent acknowledged the concern and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Joyce, called to schedule a routine air conditioning maintenance appointment. The agent confirmed details including the user's ownership status, service address, and preferred date and time, offering available slots on June 9th and later accommodating a request for June 11th. The call ended after confirming the user's preferences and checking availability.

The user received a missed call from Budget but did not have a ZIP code to provide for service. The agent attempted to transfer the call to a live agent for further assistance. The user hung up shortly after the transfer attempt.

Betty called to report that her air conditioning is not putting out cold air and requested a service appointment. The agent booked a technician visit for Wednesday, June 3rd at 10 AM and noted Betty's difficulty with heat and mobility. Betty provided positive feedback about the call experience.

Sheila from American Home Shield called Budget to provide dispatch details and requested to speak with a live representative to confirm the status of a technician appointment. The agent collected the dispatch number, service address, and customer name, then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Carlotta Jethro called to follow up on an appointment scheduled for June 1st between 12 PM and 2 PM. The agent confirmed the appointment details and assured Carlotta that her questions about the technician's call time and waiting concerns would be forwarded to the team for a follow-up. The call ended after confirming no further questions.
The user requested to speak with a service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

JD from American Home Shield called to inform Budget about an installation issue related to dispatch order 28358079, confirmed the service address and contact details, and described the technician's findings regarding the condenser fan motor and blade. The agent acknowledged the issue and assured JD that the team would be notified for follow-up. The call ended with no further questions from JD.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Melissa informed the agent that a technician left some equipment and hoses in her driveway. The agent acknowledged the issue and assured Melissa that someone from the team would follow up to arrange pickup. The call ended with confirmation of the details and no further assistance requested.

The user, Carlotta Jethro, called to confirm an air conditioner appointment scheduled for today between 12 PM and 2 PM. The agent verified the appointment details and then transferred the call to a live representative upon the user's request. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user called to report that a technician left some hoses behind and requested to speak with a technician about them. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live technician for further assistance.
The user called to return a message to Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent who could assist and successfully transferred the call.

Alex Booker called to schedule a home warranty service appointment for a cooling system that is not cooling. The agent collected all necessary details including service address, contact information, and preferred appointment time, but no dispatch number was available. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, and the call ended successfully with a promise of follow-up from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing.

Terrence called to register a cooling service request under his home warranty for his residence at 15405 Cousteau Drive, Florissant. The agent confirmed the details, including the issue of the cooling system not blowing cool air and scheduled a morning appointment for Thursday the eleventh. The call concluded with no further questions or feedback from Terrence.
The user requested to speak with Jacob Kratch this afternoon, and the agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate live agent.

The call was initiated by the user but there was no meaningful conversation or assistance provided as the user did not respond to the agent's offers to help. The call ended abruptly with the user hanging up.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.
The user requested to be transferred to a human agent immediately after the initial greeting. The agent promptly initiated a cold transfer to a live agent as requested.

The user called to inquire about a furnace repair and was waiting for confirmation on a part and repair schedule. The agent attempted to gather more details but the user requested to speak to a customer service representative. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to request cooling service in Illinois and provided their ZIP code. The agent attempted to gather more information but the user requested to speak with a representative, leading to a call transfer to a live agent.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

Wallace called to schedule a regular yearly air conditioning tune-up directly with Budget. The agent confirmed the appointment for the morning of June 11th at 10 AM and provisionally booked the service. The call ended with Wallace declining to provide feedback.

The user called to request a plumber to replace a tub drain due to paint causing a slow drain. The agent collected the user's name and attempted to clarify the user's location before transferring the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called Budget and requested to speak with a representative. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after the agent greeted the user and started to speak. No meaningful conversation or assistance took place.

The user called to check if their service request from American Home Shield was received and mentioned having a technician visit. The agent acknowledged the issue, apologized for the inconvenience, and transferred the call to the billing team to address the user's payment concern.

The user called to follow up on a home warranty appointment for furnace inspection that had not been scheduled yet. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The agent attempted to engage the user, Becky, regarding heating, cooling, or plumbing services, but the user provided minimal responses and the call ended shortly after without any service request or detailed conversation.
